tags.name: Getting Started
tags.description:
FareHarbor offers powerful APIs and webhooks designed to streamline integrations, increase automation, and scale your business--whether you're an affiliate aiming to integrate live booking capabilities, or a software partner enhancing backend operations.

### Who Can Access the API?

FareHarbor offers API access to:

- **Affiliates** looking to sell FareHarbor client inventory directly within their own platforms
- **Software Partners** integrating FareHarbor into systems like CRMs, POS, accounting tools, or waiver and photo platforms

All requests are reviewed for eligibility. To begin, email [support@fareharbor.com](mailto:support@fareharbor.com) with a brief summary of your use case. If you are a distribution affiliate, please fill out our application for API [here](https://airtable.com/appn3FgNIepgAu6GD/shra6tuh90diXXOFr).

### For Affiliates

Affiliates can use our External API to access and book inventory in real time. This enables platforms like OTAs, concierges, and marketplaces to create seamless booking experiences.

#### Key Capabilities:

- **API Access:** Available to qualified Affiliates for distribution purposes
- **Query Inventory:** Retrieve real-time availability, pricing, and item metadata ("items") for operators in your partner network or through the FareHarbor Distribution Network (FHDN)
- **Create and Manage Bookings:** Use the Bookings endpoints to create and manage bookings and manage customer data
- **Testing Environment:** Safely test your implementation in our demo environment

### For Software Partners

Software and integration partners can connect with FareHarbor to automate workflows and synchronize client data.

#### Typical Use Cases:

- CRMs, POS systems, accounting tools
- Waiver and photo capture platforms
- Support and marketing automation tools

#### Integration Capabilities:

- **Webhook Access:** Built for Software Partners to support non-distribution use cases
- **Real-Time Sync:** Configure webhooks so that you receive automatic updates on bookings, customers, and payments
- **Data Management:** Use the External API to pull booking, availability, and customer records
- **Custom Integrations:** Build tailored workflows using booking-level data, custom fields, and waiver metadata

### Resources and Support

Both affiliates and software partners have access to the following tools and documentation to support their FareHarbor integrations:

- **Production API Documentation:** [https://developer.fareharbor.com/](https://developer.fareharbor.com/) — Contains all necessary technical details about endpoints, data formats, and sample code
- **Demo API Environment:** [https://demo.fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/](https://demo.fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/) — A testing platform where you can safely experiment with API calls without affecting live data
- **Help Center:** [https://fareharbor.com/help/](https://fareharbor.com/help/) — Guidance and support articles for technical and operational questions
- **Technical Support:** Our experienced support team is ready to assist you with any questions or challenges during your integration process

Get started today to enhance your platform's capabilities and provide a seamless experience to your users with FareHarbor's comprehensive integration solutions.

## API Certification

To receive your live API credentials and become a FareHarbor API Partner, you must complete the following certification process. Remember, our test environment runs on a dedicated, scaled down infrastructure. During development and testing, do not send more than four concurrent External API requests. This certification is designed to ensure that you have thoroughly tested the integration and are able to effectively book into a variety of FareHarbor Dashboard set-ups. Once you have completed the certification, we will provide you with a survey, followed by your live API keys.

Should you have any questions, please feel free to contact your FareHarbor representative.

### Test through the API:

1. Make at least 2 bookings per charter (if there is more than one and for each User Key) across two different items:
    * Within one of these bookings, make sure to include all customer information:  customer name, phone and email.
    * Pass through a booking note in a booking.
    * Within your bookings, please make sure to send through two different customer types, if applicable.
1. Rebook one of the bookings.
1. Cancel one of the bookings.
1. If you are connecting to custom fields, pass through two different custom fields (e.g. dropdown).
1. If you are connecting to the transportation module, pass through two bookings with two different lodgings.


## Rate Limits
We enforce two rate limits on requests to the FareHarbor External API. Both are applied per IP address, not per app key.

- At most 30 requests per second per IP.
- At most 3,000 requests per 5-minute period per IP.

If multiple app keys are being used from the same IP, their requests all count toward the same pool. Going over either limit will result in an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests or 403 Forbidden response until the time window resets.

To avoid hitting these thresholds, partners should spread requests evenly across time rather than sending large batches all at once.

## Timeouts

Certain requests to the FareHarbor External API are especially time-intensive, such as some of the [Availabilities endpoints](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints).

Our infrastructure enforces an upper bound of 60 seconds on the duration of all requests to our endpoints.  When a request exceeds this limit, it will be terminated with a `504 Gateway Timeout` HTTP status code.

If you experience a 504 timeout for an availability request (such as by date range), we recommend re-trying the request after shortening the requested date range.

## TLS Version

As of 2020-09-01 we require clients that connect to our API to make use of TLS1.2 in order to ensure a speedy and secure connection. You can test this by running a curl command against our API and if you see something like `SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL in connection to fareharbor.com:443` or `Unknown SSL protocol error in connection` you are trying to use a TLS version we do not support. If that is the case it is likely that you will need to upgrade your local openssl version, which is outside the scope of this document.

## Determining User Keys

FareHarbor will provide approved API partners with User Keys that can be used to make requests and create bookings for suppliers.
The supplier must confirm their approval of your API access before you are able to make bookings for them via API.
If you are using the FareHarbor API to book for suppliers across multiple countries/currencies, you may be provided with one User Key per currency.
You can find more information about the authentication process [here](#tag/Authentication). Alternatively, you may contact <support@fareharbor.com> for assistance with User Keys.

## Structuring Requests and Responses

All requests and responses are simple JSON objects; see [`Format`](#tag/Format).

## Interacting with the API

The FareHarbor API provides a powerful way to manage bookings, customers, and inventory programmatically. Here's a quick start guide to help you set up and begin integrating with the FareHarbor API.

### Step 1: Obtain API Credentials

Register for API Access: Contact FareHarbor to request API access. This typically involves setting up an account and verifying your business details.

Receive API Keys: Once approved, you will be provided with API keys which are essential for authenticating requests.

### Step 2: Set Up Your Environment

Choose an API Client: Decide on a programming environment and API client that suits your development needs (e.g., Postman, cURL, or any HTTP client libraries available for your preferred programming language).

Install Necessary Libraries: For example, if you're using Python, you might use requests library to handle API calls.

### Step 3: Making Your First API Call

Test API Connection: Start by making a simple API request to ensure your API keys are working correctly. A typical first request might be to retrieve information about available companies.

Request:

    curl -X GET 'https://fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/companies/'
    -H "X-FareHarbor-API-App: APP-KEY"
    -H "X-FareHarbor-API-User: USER-KEY"

Response:

    {
      "companies": [
        {
          'name': 'XYZ Scuba',
          'shortname': 'xyz'
        }
      ]
    }


### Step 4: Common API Interactions

Retrieve a list of available companies: The first thing you might want to do is retrieve the list of companies that a given affiliate has access to in FareHarbor. See [`Companies`](#tag/Company-Endpoints) to learn more.

Retrieve a list of bookable items: Next you can identify all of the items on a particular provider that a given affiliate can book. Continuing the above example, we can determine which items on "XYZ Scuba" the user identified by USER-KEY can book. See [`Items`](#tag/Items-Endpoints) to learn more. 

Retrieve a list of availability for an item: Having found an item to book, you can access its availability on a per-day basis. See [`Availabilities`](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints) to learn more. 

Create a booking for an availability: Once you've found a bookable availability you can book it by simply POSTing to the availability's booking collection. See [`Bookings`](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints) to learn more.

Retrieve a booking: Given a booking by uuid it's straightforward to access the booking details.  See [`Get Booking`](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/get-booking) to learn more. 

### Step 5: Handling API Responses

Check Response Status: Ensure each API call successfully completes by checking the status codes and response messages.

Parse Data: Extract relevant information from the JSON responses for use within your application.

### Step 6: Automate and Scale

Automate Frequent Tasks: Develop scripts or applications that automate common tasks, such as getting availability or fetching regular booking reports.

Handle Errors: Implement robust error handling to deal with potential issues like network errors, API limits, or data inconsistencies.

### Step 8: Security and Compliance

Secure API Keys: Protect your API keys to prevent unauthorized access.

Data Protection: Follow best practices for data protection and ensure compliance with relevant legal regulations such as GDPR (if operating in Europe) or others specific to your location and industry.

### Step 8: Explore Advanced Features

Webhooks: Use FareHarbor's webhook system for real-time updates on bookings and availability changes. See [`Webhooks`](#tag/Webhooks) to learn more.

Custom Integration: Depending on your needs, explore custom integrations that go beyond the basic functionalities, such as integrating with third-party CRM or financial software.


### Documentation and Support

Refer to the Official Documentation: Regularly consult the FareHarbor API documentation for detailed advice on different API endpoints and updates.

Seek Support When Needed: Utilize FareHarbor’s developer support for troubleshooting and guidance on complex integration scenarios.

tags.name: Availabilities Endpoints
tags.description:
The `/availabilities` endpoint is used to retrieve all bookable availabilities for a single item within a specified date or date range. The request pulls availability data from the Item calendar of the FareHarbor dashboard. Each individual availability shows a unique PK, which is then used during the booking process, as well as a summary of its related settings, including start/end times, capacity, valid ticket options and their prices, custom fields, party size restrictions, and more.

Here the API Partner can gather the relevant PKs that they need to create their booking, and they can use the data on the API to connect and display the bookable dates and times on the product calendars on their end. The more frequently they hit this endpoint, the more accurate their product calendars will be in respect to FareHarbor.

Online Booking Status: Each availability has an online booking status, which controls how it can be viewed and booked by online customers. In most cases, the status will be set to `auto`, which automatically prevents online customers from booking after a certain time cutoff, based on [item’s settings](https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/items/online-booking-settings/).

For a complete description of these statuses and their meaning, go to [https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/availabilities/updating/online-booking-status/](https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/availabilities/updating/online-booking-status/).

**Things to Note:**

*   API Partners may pull availability at whatever intervals they want, as long as they fall within the rate limit, but we generally recommend that, at minimum, partners pull availability for the next 90 days (in 7 day segments) once per week, for the next  30 days once per day, and for the current day every 2 hours.

*   The standard availability endpoints will return the fully detailed availability, including custom field information and headlines. If you are not connecting to custom fields, you can include `/minimal` in your request to exclude custom field information from the response. This can also help with performance speed.

*   We are constantly monitoring our API and optimizing for performance. Depending on the complexity of certain products and how they are configured in our system, availability requests for some products may take longer than others. In these cases it helps to reduce the date range being pulled.
* Availability Restrictions:
    * FareHarbor supports optional party size booking restrictions at the availability level. The following properties will be included for each availability provided by the availability endpoints to indicate applicable restrictions:
        * `minimum_party_size`: minimum number of customers currently allowed on a booking.
        * `maximum_party_size`: maximum number of customers currently allowed on a booking.
    * Note: a number value will be provided for enabled restrictions; a null value will be provided for restrictions that are not enabled.
* Customer Type Rate Restrictions:
    * FareHarbor also supports optional party size booking restrictions at the customer type rate level. The following properties will be included for each customer type rate provided by availability endpoints to indicate applicable restrictions:
        * `minimum_party_size`: minimum number of the associated customer type allowed in a party.
        * `maximum_party_size`: maximum number of the associated customer type allowed in a party.
    * Note: the minimum_party_size restriction only applies when at least one customer of the associated customer type is in the party.
    * Note: a number value will be provided for enabled restrictions; a null value will be provided for restrictions that are not enabled.
* Availability start and end times are in the company's local timezone.  When providing a date or date range for availabilities, those dates are taken as local time of the company.

tags.name: Bookings Endpoints
tags.description:
The bookings endpoints in the FareHarbor API provide comprehensive functionalities for managing reservations efficiently. These endpoints enable developers and business owners to integrate and automate booking-related operations seamlessly into their services. Through these endpoints, users can create, retrieve, update, and cancel bookings directly through the API.

Key functionalities offered by the bookings endpoints include:
* **Creating Bookings:** Enables the creation of new bookings, allowing customers to reserve spots for tours and activities directly through third-party applications.
* **Retrieving Bookings:** Offers the ability to query and retrieve details of existing bookings, providing essential information like booking status, customer details, and payment information.
* **Updating Bookings:** Supports modifications to existing bookings, which is crucial for managing changes in customer plans.
* **Cancelling Bookings:** Allows for the cancellation of bookings, helping to manage availability and customer expectations effectively.

These endpoints are designed to foster a more connected experience between tour and activity operators and their customers by simplifying the booking process through automation and integration. This ensures that businesses can focus more on providing an exceptional service experience, while the logistical aspects of bookings are handled efficiently through the API.

**Things to Note:**
* Orders:
    * Orders are a way of grouping collections of bookings so that they can be created or cancelled as a block.
    * A booking can belong to an order, but it doesn't have to. An order can include any number of bookings.
    * When `order` is not `null`, `order` is an object that provides the following property:
        * `display_id`: `string`  - A unique identifier for the order.
    * See [Orders](#tag/Orders) to learn more about creating and retrieving orders.

tags.name: Checkin Endpoints
tags.description:
The check-in endpoints in the FareHarbor API provide essential capabilities for managing the check-in processes efficiently and seamlessly. These endpoints are crucial for businesses in the tour and activity sector, as they allow for real-time, automated check-in operations through integrated systems.

Key functionalities offered by the check-in endpoints include:

* **Checking In Participants:** Automates the process of marking customers as present for an activity, streamlining operations and reducing manual errors.
* **Listing Check-In Status:** Provides the ability to pull information on who has checked in and who hasn't, ensuring staff are well-informed and can make necessary arrangements.
* **Updating Check-In Records:** Supports modifications to check-in records, which can be crucial for managing late arrivals or last-minute changes in customer plans.
* **Integration with Scanning Devices:** Some endpoints are designed to work with barcode or QR code scanning devices to facilitate faster and more reliable check-in procedures.

Integrating these check-in endpoints into business systems helps in providing a smoother and more efficient customer service experience, minimizing wait times, and allowing staff to focus on customer interaction rather than manual check-in processes. By leveraging these APIs, businesses can enhance operational efficiency and improve customer satisfaction.

tags.name: Company Endpoints
tags.description:
The Company endpoints in the FareHarbor API provide robust functionalities for accessing and managing information related to companies that offer tours and activities. These endpoints are crucial for developers who need to integrate company-specific data into their applications, ensuring that users have up-to-date and detailed information about the services they are interested in.

Key functionalities offered by the Company endpoints include:
* **Retrieving Company Information:** Enables access to detailed information about a company, including its description, contact details, and available locations. This is vital for providing comprehensive data to users looking to book activities or tours.
* **Listing Services Offered:** Allows for the enumeration of all services and activities offered by a company, which is essential for users to find and book relevant offerings.
* **Accessing Operational Details:** Provides insights into the operational aspects of a company, such as business hours, peak times, and special events, assisting in better planning and resource management.

These endpoints serve as a critical connection between companies in the tour and activity sectors and their potential customers, facilitating seamless information sharing and service discovery. By leveraging these API endpoints, businesses can offer enriched, detailed, and updated data to users, which enhances transparency and trust while promoting ease of access to company offerings.

tags.name: Items Endpoints
tags.description:
The Items endpoints in the FareHarbor API provide comprehensive access to detailed information about the various activities and services offered by a company. These endpoints are essential for any application that needs to integrate, display, or manage bookings for specific items or activities within a company's portfolio.

Key functionalities offered by the Items endpoints include:
* **Retrieving Item Details:** Facilitates access to in-depth information about each item, including descriptions, pricing, availability, and any special requirements or features associated with the item.
* **Listing All Items:** Allows users to obtain a list of all available items offered by a company, making it easier to browse and select suitable options based on preferences and requirements.
* **Item Availability:** Provides endpoints to check the availability of items, helping to ensure that the booking process is up-to-date and accurate, preventing overbookings and scheduling conflicts.
* **Customizable Responses:** Supports filtering and customization of the data returned, enabling developers to tailor the API responses to specific needs and ensure that the integration feels native to the application.

By integrating these Items endpoints, developers can enhance their applications with powerful, real-time access to activity data, ensuring that users receive a seamless, accurate, and engaging booking experience. This integration is crucial for facilitating efficient and effective interactions between customers and tour or activity providers.

tags.name: Crew Members Endpoints
tags.description:
The Crew Members endpoints in the FareHarbor API provide comprehensive functionality for managing crew assignments on availabilities. These endpoints allow you to assign, update, and remove crew members from specific availability slots, ensuring proper staffing and crew coordination for tours and activities.

Key functionalities offered by the Crew Members endpoints include:
* **Listing Crew Members:** Retrieve all crew members currently assigned to a specific availability, including their roles and notes.
* **Adding Crew Members:** Assign users to availabilities as crew members with optional role assignments and notes.
* **Updating Crew Members:** Modify existing crew member assignments, specifically changing roles or updating notes.
* **Removing Crew Members:** Remove crew members from availabilities when they are no longer needed.

These endpoints are essential for tour operators who need to manage staffing levels, assign specific roles to crew members, and maintain clear communication about crew responsibilities through notes and role assignments.

tags.name: Users Endpoints
tags.description: The Users endpoints provide access to user information within the context of company operations, specifically for crew management purposes.

tags.name: Roles Endpoints
tags.description: The Roles endpoints provide access to role definitions within companies, enabling structured crew member assignments with specific responsibilities and permissions.

tags.name: Lodging Endpoints
tags.description: With our `/lodgings` endpoints our API Partners are able to retrieve our charters’ lodging options, both on a dashboard level as well as on the availability level, and offer these for customers to choose from when creating a booking.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
An app key and a user key are required to authenticate all requests to
the FareHarbor external API.  The app key identifies your application,
and can be used for all of your affiliates. The user key identifies a specific
user on a specific affiliate.

These keys are in UUID format. Note that these keys *must* be kept private. 
If your key is disclosed publicly please contact support@fareharbor.com to have it rotated.

## Keys sent as headers (recommended)

The recommended approach to passing your keys is via the custom headers
`X-FareHarbor-API-App` and `X-FareHarbor-API-User`.

```
GET /api/external/v1/companies/ HTTP/1.1
Host: fareharbor.com
X-FareHarbor-API-App: APP-KEY
X-FareHarbor-API-User: USER-KEY

$ curl -H "X-FareHarbor-API-App: APP-KEY" -H "X-FareHarbor-API-User: USER-KEY" https://fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/companies/
```

## Requesting Keys
If you would like to pursue an integration with the FareHarbor API, please contact strategicpartnerships@fareharbor.com so that we can review your request.

Note that we cannot grant access in all cases.

tags.name: Cancellation Policies
tags.description:
In order for cancellations to be made via API, all connections must be assigned a technical cancellation policy in the supplier’s settings in FareHarbor. As a standard step in the enablement process, we assign the **Default - API** cancellation policy when we set up every new API partner with access to mutual suppliers.

*The Default - API cancellation policy allows for a booking to be canceled up until the availability start time (which are given in company local time). If a cancellation is attempted through the API for a booking after the exact start time of that availability has passed, it will be met with an error.*

This allows the API partner to set cancellation policies and restrictions in their own system, and  regardless of the cancellation policy agreed to between the API partner and the supplier (ex. 72 hours, 24 hours, 2 hours) our system will always allow the cancellation as long as the start time of that availability has not yet passed. We do this to allow API partners to implement cancellation restrictions on their end and to defer to their policy for when they allow their customer to cancel.

**Grace Period**

Regardless of any cancellation policy, the FareHarbor API will allow the immediate cancellation of a booking if it is requested within 5 minutes.

**Disclaimer**

API Partners should discuss and determine the cancellation terms for bookings made in their partnerships with suppliers, and the API Partner should have the ability to enforce them in their integrations. The Default API cancellation policy is intentionally open so that FareHarbor can always facilitate the cancellation and keep the supplier's availabilities up to date, but it should not be interpreted as the supplier’s "official" policy. Just because this default setting will technically allow the cancellation request up until the start time does not mean the supplier allows it.

## Understanding Cancellation Policy Fields in the API

Cancellation policies are referenced in a few different ways, which we will aim to make clear here.

Firstly, the Default API cancellation policy that we have been describing here is exposed on the **[Detailed Items](#tag/Items-Endpoints/operation/get-items)** endpoint (when `detailed=yes` is specified in the query string) as the `effective_cancellation_policy`. It indicates that the API will allow a cancellation up until 0.0 hours before the start of the availability.

API Partners should **not** import this field, as it only reflects the default setting across all integrations and does not reflect the supplier’s preferences or any policy that they have agreed to with the API Partner.

Separately on the **Items** endpoint you will find the `cancellation_policy` field, where the supplier may input their own cancellation information as text.

```
"cancellation_policy": "A full refund will be issued if notice is given at least 24 hours before start time.",
```

This is where the supplier may describe their cancellation policies and processes, however it is important to note that this field is not required for suppliers, so it may be blank. It is also important to note that whatever is written in the `cancellation_policy`  field is what suppliers display on their own website - so it is not customized for API Partners.

If you receive an unexpected `400 booking-cancellation-error` code, you may contact api-support@fareharbor.com to confirm the cancellation settings and see if any changes are needed.

## Items Endpoint

When `detailed=yes` is specified as the query string for the items endpoint, an `effective_cancellation_policy` property will be provided for each item that specifies data regarding the effective cancellation policy as shown in the responses to the following endpoints:

* [GET items](#tag/Items-Endpoints/operation/get-items)
* [GET item](#tag/Items-Endpoints/operation/get-item).

The `type` property specifies the effective cancellation policy type. The following types are supported:

* `hours-before-start` - bookings are eligible for cancellation some number of hours before availability start time.
* `hours-before-midnight` - bookings are eligible for cancellation some number of hours before midnight on availability start date.
* `always` - bookings are eligible for cancellation up until a cutoff that is determined by the availability (usually the availability start time).
* `never` - bookings are never eligible for cancellation (e.g. no effective cancellation policy, insufficient privileges, cancellation disallowed by policy).

The `cutoff_hours_before` property specifies the number of hours for the `hours-before-start` and `hours-before-midnight` types. Note: this value can be negative indicating that bookings can be cancelled some number of hours after availability start time (when type is `hours-before-start`) or some number of hours after midnight on availability start date (when type is `hours-before-midnight`). The `cutoff_hours_before` property provides `null` when no cutoff is applicable (when bookings are never eligible for cancellation).

## Availability Endpoints

When `detailed=yes` is specified as the query string for the availabilities endpoints, an `effective_cancellation_policy` property is provided for each availability that specifies data regarding the effective cancellation policy as shown in the responses of the following availabilities endpoints:

* [GET availabilities by date](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints/operation/get-minimal-availabilities-by-date)
* [GET availabilities by date range](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints/operation/get-minimal-availabilities-by-date-range)
The `type` property specifies the effective cancellation policy type (see the items endpoint section for more details).

The `cutoff` property specifies the last possible time (ISO8601 timestamp in company local time) bookings are eligible for cancellation. The `cutoff` property provides `null` when no cutoff is applicable (when bookings are never eligible for cancellation).

## Retrieve Booking Endpoint

An `is_eligible_for_cancellation` and `effective_cancellation_policy` property is provided in the response to the following booking endpoints:

* [GET booking](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/get-booking)
* [DELETE booking](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/delete-booking)
* [PUT booking note](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/put-update-booking-note)
* [POST bookings](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/post-bookings)

The `is_eligible_for_cancellation` property provides a boolean value indicating whether the booking is currently eligible for cancellation.

The `type` property specifies the effective cancellation policy type (see the items endpoint section for more details).

The `cutoff` property specifies the last possible time (ISO8601 timestamp in company local time) the booking is eligible for cancellation. The `cutoff` property provides `null` when no cutoff is applicable (when the booking is never eligible for cancellation).

tags.name: Custom Fields
Data Types
tags.description:
# Fields ## Markdown fields Many of the fields returned by the API contain user-defined Markdown. We also return the rendered HTML version of the field. In the following schemas, fields of type `markdown` actually contain a `string`, and imply a second field in the schema containing the rendered HTML. So the following: * `field`: `markdown` Should be treated as if it were: * `field`: `string` * `field_safe_html`: `string` The "safe" part of the field name indicates that only a limited subset of HTML is allowed; all other HTML entities are escaped. ## Amount fields All currency amounts are returned as an integer number of the smallest negotiable unit of money in the company's currency. In the case of USD, this means USD cents. So, for instance, `1425` means $14.25 USD. Some amount fields may have corresponding fields whose names are suffixed with `_display`. These fields will contain strings representing the same amount of money, but using the customary formatting for the company's country. For instance, in the case of US/USD, this will be the string `14.25`. Note that some countries may use a comma rather than a period as the decimal point. * `field`: `number` * `field_display`: `string` ## Datetime fields All datetimes are returned in ISO8601 format; for example `2013-02-04T06:21:36+0000` * `field`: `string` # Schemas ## Address Structured address data. * `city`: `string` * `country`: `string` * `postal_code`: `string` * `province`: `string` * `street`: `string` Example: { "city": "Honolulu", "country: "US", "postal_code": "96821", "province": "HI", "street": "123 Wailupe Cir" } ## Company ### Minimal Representation A `Company` object represent an affiliate or provider. * `shortname`: `string` The company's ID. * `name`: `string` The company's name. * `currency`: `string` The company's currency. Both capacities, if specified, cannot be exceeded, despite the fact that the sum of available capacities of the customer type rates may not match the available capacity of the availability itself. ## Extended Option Extended options are applicable to custom fields of type `extended-option`. * `pk`: `number` The extended option's unique ID. * `name`: `string` The extended option's name. * `description`: `markdown` The extended option's description. * `modifier_kind`: `string` Can be `offset` or `percentage`. Determines whether an offset or percentage is used when the total is adjusted or set. * `modifier_type`: `string` Can be `adjust` or `set`. Determines whether the total is adjusted or set. * `offset`: `amount` When `modifier_type` is `adjust`: a positive or negative amount value. When `modifier_type` is `set`: a positive amount value. Only applicable when `modifier_kind` is `offset`. * `percentage`: `number` When `modifier_type` is `adjust`: a number between -100 and 100 inclusive. When `modifier_type` is `set`: a number between 0 and 100 inclusive. Only applicable when `modifier_kind` is `percentage`. * `is_always_per_customer`: `bool` Whether pricing should be applied to each customer (when the customer field is at the booking level). * `is_taxable`: `bool` Whether the cost of this option should affect the taxable total of a booking. Example: { "pk": 105, "name": "Helmet", "description": "Need a helmet?", "modifier_kind": "percentage", "modifier_type": "adjust", "offset": null, "percentage": 35, "is_always_per_customer": true, "is_taxable": false } ## Custom Field * `pk`: `number` The custom field's unique ID. * `type`: `string` The custom field's type. Supported types: `yes-no`, `short`, `long`, and `extended-option`. * `is_required`: `bool` Indicates whether the custom field requires a value. * `description`: `markdown` The custom field's description. * `name`: `string` The name of the custom field. * `booking_notes`: `markdown` Notes for the customer specific to the custom field. * `offset`: `amount` A positive or negative amount value. * `extended_options`: `[ ExtendedOption ]` A list the custom field's extended options. This will only be provided for custom fields that have type `extended-option`. Example: { "pk": 4502, "type": "extended-option" "is_required": false, "description": "Do you need additional safety equipment?", "name": "Safety Equipment", "offset": 100, "extended_options": { "pk": 105, "name": "Helmet", "description": "Need a helmet?", "modifier_kind": "percentage", "modifier_type": "adjust", "offset": null, "percentage": 35, "is_always_per_customer": true, "is_taxable": false } } ## Custom Field Instance * `pk`: `number` The custom field instance's unique ID. * `custom_field`: `CustomField` The custom field associated with the custom field instance. Example: { "pk": 2681, "custom_field": { "pk": 4502, "type": "yes-no" "is_required": false, "description": "Do you need additional safety equipment?", "name": "Safety Equipment", "offset": 100, "extended_options": { "pk": 105, "name": "Helmet", "description": "Need a helmet?", "modifier_kind": "percentage", "modifier_type": "adjust", "offset": null, "percentage": 35, "is_always_per_customer": true, "is_taxable": false } } } ## Custom Field Value * `custom_field`: `CustomField.pk` A valid Custom Field pk. * `value`: `string` A string up to 2048 characters. If the custom field is of type `yes-no`, "true" or "false" must be specified. If the custom field is of type `extended-option`, an extended option pk must be specified. Example: { "custom_field": 4502, "value": "false", "extended-option": 105 } ## Crew Member A `CrewMember` object represents a user assigned to work on a specific availability. Crew members can have assigned roles and notes that provide context about their responsibilities or special instructions. * `pk`: `integer` The crew member's unique identifier. This ID is specific to the crew member assignment and can be used for updating or removing the crew member from the availability. * `user`: `User` The user assigned as a crew member. Contains the user's name and username for identification purposes. * `role`: `Role | null` The role assigned to the crew member, if any. Roles help categorize crew members and define their responsibilities within the organization. This field is optional and can be null if no specific role is assigned. * `note`: `string` A text note providing additional context, instructions, or information about the crew member's assignment. This can include special responsibilities, equipment assignments, or any other relevant details. Example: { "crew_member": { "pk": 12345, "user": { "name": "John Smith", "username": "jsmith" }, "role": { "pk": 1, "short_name": "captain" }, "note": "Responsible for safety briefing and equipment check" } } Example with no role assigned: { "crew_member": { "pk": 12346, "user": { "name": "Jane Doe", "username": "jdoe" }, "role": null, "note": "General crew member" } } ## User A `User` object represents a person who can be assigned as a crew member to availabilities. Users are identified by their name and username within the system. * `name`: `string` The user's full name as it appears in the system. This is typically the user's first and last name combined and is used for display purposes in crew rosters and assignments. * `username`: `string` The user's unique username within the system. This is used for identification and assignment purposes when adding crew members to availabilities. The username must be unique across the system. Example: { "user": { "name": "John Smith", "username": "jsmith" } } Example in crew member context: { "users": [ { "name": "John Smith", "username": "jsmith" }, { "name": "Jane Doe", "username": "jdoe" }, { "name": "Mike Johnson", "username": "mjohnson" } ] } ## Role A `Role` object represents a specific position or responsibility that can be assigned to crew members. Roles help organize crew members by their duties and responsibilities within the company's operations. * `pk`: `integer` The role's unique identifier. This ID is used when assigning roles to crew members and for referencing the role in other operations. * `short_name`: `string` The role's short name or title. This is a concise identifier for the role that is used in crew member assignments and displays. Examples might include "captain", "guide", "safety_officer", or "mechanic". Example: { "role": { "pk": 1, "short_name": "captain" } } Example in roles list context: { "roles": [ { "pk": 1, "short_name": "captain" }, { "pk": 2, "short_name": "guide" }, { "pk": 3, "short_name": "safety_officer" }, { "pk": 4, "short_name": "mechanic" } ] } Example in crew member context: { "crew_member": { "pk": 12345, "user": { "name": "John Smith", "username": "jsmith" }, "role": { "pk": 1, "short_name": "captain" }, "note": "Responsible for safety briefing and equipment check" } }

tags.name: Examples
Format
tags.description:
# Interchange Format

All interactions with the API are done via JSON.

# Requests

## Request Body

Some requests (particularly the `POST` to create a booking) require a body.
The body should consist entirely of a JSON object.

## Authentication

You'll need to authenticate every request you make the API; see [authentication](#tag/Authentication).

# Responses

## General Structure

The body of every response is a valid JSON object (and in particular *not* a JSON array,
number, string, and so on).

### Success responses

Successful responses contain the data requested.  The structure of these responses is described in detail
in [data-types](#tag/Data-Types) however in general they follow this structure:

* `<singular type>`: `object`

or

* `<plural type>`: `array`

Examples:

```
    {
      "company": {
        "shortname": "hawaiianadventures",
        "name": "Hawaiian Adventures"
      }
    }
```

```
    {
      "companies": [
        {
          "shortname": "hawaiianadventures",
          "name": "Hawaiian Adventures"
        }
      ]
    }
```

### Error responses

* `error`: `string`

A message describing the error.

* `status`: `number`

The HTTP status code for the response.

Example:

    {
      "error": "Method not allowed",
      "status": 405
    }

Assuming that `response` contains the entire JSON response, a response is an error when `response.error`
is defined; otherwise it is a success.

## HTTP Codes

We use the usual HTTP statuses to indicate success:

* 200: Succcess

* 201: Created

A `2xx` response is always indicative of a successful request.

We also use HTTP error codes to indicate the usual sorts of errors; the following
are the ones you are most likely to encounter.

* 400: Bad Request

  The request was invalid and could not be processed.

  This is most likely to occur when attempting to create a booking,
  and can correspond to things like attempting to overbook an availability
  or providing invalid data like customer name, phone number, etc.

* 403: Forbidden

  You do not have permission to access this endpoint in this way.

  Review [authentication](#tag/Authentication) and verify that you are sending the
  correct API keys, and that you do indeed have permission to access to
  endpoint you are attempting to access.

* 404: Not Found

  The company, item, or availability does not exist; it may have been
  deleted or otherwise become unavailable, or you may have an incorrect ID.

* 405: Method Not Allowed

  You are trying to use an unsupported HTTP method (`GET`, `POST`, and so on)
  with this endpoint.

* 420: Enhance Your Calm

  Returned when a request has been rate-limited due to too many concurrent
  connections. It is *very* unlikely that you will encounter this in normal
  usage.

* 500: Internal Server Error

  An unexpected error occured.

* 503: Service Unavailable

  FareHarbor is temporarily unavailable, but will return shortly. Try again later.

A `4xx` request is always indicative of an problem with the request being made,
while a `5xx` error indicates a server-side error.

A detailed list of error codes and messages are described in [error-codes](#tag/Error-Codes)

tags.name: Orders
tags.description:
Multiple bookings can be grouped into an **Order**.  This feature is beneficial for API Partners and Affiliates who want to offer bundled experiences to increase guest satisfaction and revenue.  For example, they can allow guests to book multiple activities at once or to create custom packages booking activities one by one.  This can increase operational efficiency for activity providers.

# General Outline of the Creation of an Order
An **Order** represents a bundle of **Bookings**, and every booking is created the same way (whether as part of an order or not), with one crucial difference.  In every case, the `POST` **Bookings** endpoint is used (see [Bookings](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/post-bookings)) following the flow detailed below:

1. Make the first booking.
2. Save the `uuid` field value from the response.
3. From the second booking on, insert the `uuid` value from step 2 into the `orderize_booking` field of the request.
4. Every additional booking will have the same value in the response field `order.display_id`.  This is the order id in common to all the bookings making up the order.

### Example Response
```
{
  "booking": {
    "affiliate_company": {
      "currency": "usd",
      "name": "Test Affiliate",
      "shortname": "affiliate"
    },
    ...
    "order": { "display_id": "ABTU" },
    ...
    "source_type": "external-api",
    "status": "booked",
    "uuid": "869b0840-80ce-46a6-abf9-4fb12c7d4143",
    "voucher_number": "V1",
    "waiver_signatures": {}
  }
}
```

# Requirements
1. Before adding bookings to an order, the charter company offering the availabilities being booked must have Orders enabled in their FareHarbor dashboard under company advanced settings.
1. All availabilities booked under an order must belong to the same charter company.
1. Every booking bundled inside an order will be created for the `contact` passed in the `POST` request for the initial booking.  The `contact` in all subsequent booking `POST` requests will be ignored.

### Error Response
If the charter company does not have orders enabled, the following error will be returned:

```
{"error":"Orders not allowed","status":403,"code":"orderize-booking-uuid-invalid-error"}
```

# Retrieving an Order
The only identifier necessary for retrieving an order is the `uuid` of one of the bookings in the order.  Since the `uuid` of the first booking was used when creating the second booking and possibly all the subsequent bookings, the same `uuid` can be used for retrieval, as well.  But any of the other bookings' `uuid`s would work, as well.

The endpoint used is the normal `GET` [Booking](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/get-booking).

When the booking being retrieved is part of an order, the response will contain the following `related_bookings` data structure:

```
{...,
    related_bookings: {
       related_order: {
           id: AHWS,
           bookings: [
               uuid1,
               uuid2,
               ...
           ],
       },
    },
    ...
}
```

As this example illustrates, the booking in question belongs to `order_id: AHWS`, and the array of `bookings` below that order lists all the `uuid`s of bookings in the same order.

Currently, the API only permits the creation of a single order of related bookings.  If the retrieved booking belongs to an order, the `related_bookings` object will contain its `order_id` and a list of bookings.  Otherwise, `related_bookings` will be an empty object.

FAQ And Best Practices
tags.description:
## Question: Slow `/availabilities/date-range/` endpoint

The Availabilities Date Range endpoint takes too long to return, or
times out and never returns. What should I do?

### Answer

There are a couple of things you can do:

1. BE SURE YOU ARE USING THE MINIMAL AVAILABILITIES ENDPOINT. This is
   the one described in the documentation:
   [Availabilities Endpoint](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints)

   `GET /companies/<shortname>/items/<item.pk>/minimal/availabilities/date-range/<start-date>/<end-date>/`

   Make sure the endpoint path contains the word `minimal`.

   Other versions of this endpoint are much slower, and are no longer supported
   for new connections.

2. DECREASE THE DATE RANGE for which you are requesting availability
   data. We recommend the following guidelines:

   * For 90 days, pull in 7 day increments once per week.
   * For 30 days, pull in 7 day increments once per day.
   * For current day, pull every 2 hours.

## Question: How Do I Find Availabilities For A Certain Date?

### Answer

To find availabilities for a certain date using the FareHarbor API, follow these steps:

#### Step 1: Obtain API Credentials

Ensure you have your API credentials (App key and User key) ready, as you will need them to authenticate your requests.

#### Step 2: Identify the Item

Before checking availability, you need to know the specific item (activity or service) for which you want to check availability. You can retrieve a list of items using the [`/items`](#tag/Items-Endpoints/operation/get-items) endpoint.

#### Step 3: Make an API Request for Availability

Use the [availabilities](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints/operation/get-minimal-availabilities-by-date) endpoint to check availability for a specific item on a certain date. The request format is as follows:

**Example API Request**

```
curl -X GET 'https://fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/companies/{company_id}/minimal/items/{item_id}/availabilities/date/{date}/' \
-H "X-FareHarbor-API-App: APP-KEY" \
-H "X-FareHarbor-API-User: USER-KEY"
```

Replace the following placeholders:

* `{shortname}`: The ID of the company that offers the item.
* `{item.pk}`: The ID of the item you are checking availability for.
* `{date}`: The date you want to check availability for, formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD`.
* `APP-KEY`: Your FareHarbor App key.
* `USER-KEY`: Your FareHarbor User key.

#### Step 4: Analyze the Response

The response will contain availability information for the specified item on the given date. Here’s an example of what the response might look like:

```
{
  "availabilities": [
    {
      "start_time": "2022-12-15T09:00:00Z",
      "end_time": "2022-12-15T11:00:00Z",
    },
    {
      "start_time": "2022-12-15T13:00:00Z",
      "end_time": "2022-12-15T15:00:00Z",
    }
  ]
}
```

This indicates the available time slots and the number of spots left for each time slot.

#### Step 5: Handle Errors

Make sure to handle any potential errors in your API request, such as invalid item IDs, company IDs, or dates. Check the response status codes to ensure the request was successful.

## Question: 403 from FareHarbor External API

I am calling the FareHarbor External API. FareHarbor is responding
with a "403 Forbidden". How can I fix this?

### Answer

Double-check that you are using the right keys in your API call.

## Question: Cancelling rebooked bookings

Say I create booking A, then I rebook it to booking B, then I want to
cancel the booking. When cancelling via API, should I cancel booking A
or booking B?

### Answer

Cancelling either one via API will cause FareHarbor to cancel the
booking. Regardless of which you cancel, booking A will wind up with
`"status":"rebooked"` and booking B will wind up with
`"status":"cancelled"`. So cancel whichever you like. In many cases,
depending how your system is set up, the path of least confusion on
your side may be to CANCEL THE MOST RECENT BOOKING.

## Question: Translations

Can I use the FareHarbor External API to retrieve translated content?

### Answer

Yes. You can retrieve translated content provided by the dashboard company in any language that is "active" in their dashboard, in the Translation Languages section. To retrieve a particular language, add a `language` query parameter to the API request:

    GET /companies/<shortname>/items/?language=es

Doing this gives you company-provided translations. It does not translate field names etc., and it does not change currency formatting or provide other localization.

## Question: Online Booking Statuses

What are online bookings statuses?

### Answer

Online Booking Status: Each availability has an online booking status, which controls how it can be viewed and booked by online customers. In most cases, the status will be set to `auto`, which automatically prevents online customers from booking after a certain time cutoff, based on [item’s settings](https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/items/online-booking-settings/). The other statuses include `open`, `call`, `closed`, `cancelled`.

These statuses are included in:
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/`
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/items/<item.pk>/minimal/availabilities/date/<date>/`
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/items/<item.pk>/minimal/availabilities/date-range/<start-date>/<end-date>/`

For a complete description of these statuses and their meaning, go to [https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/availabilities/updating/online-booking-status/](https://help.fareharbor.com/dashboard/availabilities/updating/online-booking-status/).

## Question: How do I implement bi-directional crew management with FareHarbor?

### Answer

FareHarbor provides bi-directional crew management through API endpoints and webhooks:

**1. Pushing data to FareHarbor:** Use API endpoints with your app and user keys to create, update, and manage crew member assignments.

**2. Receiving data from FareHarbor:** Configure webhooks to receive real-time notifications when crew assignments change in the FareHarbor dashboard.

## Question: How do I manage crew members using the API?

### Answer

Use these three main endpoints:

**Crew Members Management:**
* `POST /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/crew-members/` - Create assignment
* `PUT /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/crew-members/<crew-member.pk>/` - Update assignment
* `DELETE /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/crew-members/<crew-member.pk>/` - Delete assignment
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/crew-members/` - Get assignments

**Users and Roles:**
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/users/` - Get all company users
* `GET /companies/<shortname>/roles/` - Get all available roles

**Example:**
```bash
curl -X POST 'https://fareharbor.com/api/external/v1/companies/hawaiianadventures/availabilities/12345/crew-members/' \
-H "X-FareHarbor-API-App: YOUR-APP-KEY" \
-H "X-FareHarbor-API-User: YOUR-USER-KEY" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{
  "user_id": 789,
  "role_id": 12,
  "note": "Experienced captain"
}'
```

## Question: How do I configure crew member webhooks?

### Answer

* In the dashboard create or select a user under which the webhooks will be congifured: in the Users page, select a user and nevigate to the tab "Webhooks".
* Click "Add Webhook".
* Fill in the webhook name.
* Select "Crew Members" schema.
* Provide your webhook URL.

After the webhook is created all the crew member updates: creating, updating, deletion will be sent to the provided URL.

**Requirements:**
* Your webhook URL must have a valid SSL certificate
* Your endpoint must return a 200 HTTP status code


**Webhook payload example:**
```json
{
```

# FH API Integration Best Practices

This section describes some best practices for integrating with FH
using webhooks and APIs.

**Notice on JSON Format Changes**

Please be aware that the JSON formats and structures used in our API & Webhooks are subject to change. We continuously work to improve our API & Webhooks to meet the evolving needs of our users and to embrace technological advancements. As a result, modifications to the data formats, including additions or deprecations, can occur.

**We strongly advise all users to design their applications in a manner that is capable of accommodating such changes.** This approach will ensure that your integration remains robust and functional, despite potential changes in data structures or schema.

## Consider using Zapier, or a similar service.

Before writing software to integrate with FareHarbor webhooks, look
into using Zapier or something similar. In many cases, doing an
integration this way will be quicker, easier, more reliable, and less
expensive than doing it yourself with your own servers and custom
software.

In order to integrate with the FareHarbor webhooks and/or API
directly, without using Zapier or the like, you will need time from
experienced software engineers. If you do not have them on staff, you
may need to contract them and manage them. This is not always easy.

## The data model

### The Booking UUID

The most important field in the booking schema is the "uuid" field. IF
YOU RECEIVE TWO WEBHOOKS WITH THE SAME UUID, THEY ARE TALKING ABOUT
THE SAME BOOKING. This is the most important thing to understand about
bookings.

You will likely receive multiple webhooks for the same booking. For
instance, you will receive one when it's created, and you may receive
another if the contact information is updated, or if a customer
checkin occurs.

For any given uuid, the most recent webhook received will contain the
most up-to-date data.

You can always retrieve the most recent details for a booking using
the Retrieve Bookings endpoint,
[Bookings Endpoint](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints):

    GET /companies/<shortname>/bookings/<Booking.uuid>/

### The Customer PK

Another important field is the "customers" field. This field contains
a list of customers. Each customer will have its own "pk". The PK is a
globally-unique identifier for the customer.

The details of a booking may change, but as long as it is not
rebooked, the customer PK will not change, and can be used as an ID
within your system, directly or indirectly.

If you receive a webhook from FareHarbor for a booking that is already
recorded in your system, this new webhook may contain updated
information. Using the PK, you can check each customer's data to see
if any piece of that customer's data has changed--the contents of a
custom field, for instance.

When a rebooking occurs, customer PKs are reassigned. For most
practical purposes, you can treat a rebooking event the same as
cancellation of the old booking and creation of the new booking. Data
associated with the old booking are no longer relevant.

## Retrieving All Existing Bookings

Using the External API, it is possible to retrieve all existing bookings.

In pseudocode, it looks like this:

    retrieve a list of companies via the [Companies endpoint](#tag/Company-Endpoints)
    for each company,
      retrieve a list of items via the [Items endpoint](#tag/Items-Endpoints)
      for each item,
        retrieve lists of availabilities via the [Availabilities endpoint](#tag/Availabilities-Endpoints) (use bookable_only=no)
        for each availability,
          retrieve a list of bookings via the [Availability Bookings endpoint](#tag/Bookings-Endpoints/operation/get-bookings)

There is sample python code to retrieve all bookings [here](#tag/Examples/Retrieve-Bookings)


## Collecting Phone Numbers

We recommend that you collect customers' phone numbers, including
country code, when creating bookings, to allow for communication
between the operator and the customer.

The phone numbers should be in the [E164 format](https://www.twilio.com/docs/glossary/what-e164)

Example POST:

    /companies/<shortname>/availabilities/<availability.pk>/bookings/

get.description:
The `/companies` endpoint lists every FareHarbor supplier that an API Partner can access and book through the API. The companies on this list are determined by which suppliers have requested the enablement of the API access for the given API Partner on their dashboard.

When this request is successful, the API partner will receive a response from our system showing all the accessible suppliers alongside the respective company names, shortnames and the currency of their accounts. 

**Things to note:**
* The value for the “shortname” will always be what is used in the subsequent endpoints in order to access a specific company’s information.
* If (in the live environment) you do not see a supplier listed on this endpoint, there could be a few reasons why that is. It could be that the supplier has not requested your enablement yet. It could also be that you made the request using one currency user key when the supplier is on a different currency. Lastly, it may be possible that the supplier has been offboarded from FareHarbor and therefore no longer accessible.
* Routine calls to this endpoint are recommended to refresh the most up to date list of available suppliers. If a company shortname is no longer available, that shortname can be removed from future /items/ and /availabilities/ requests to avoid ongoing errors.

200.description:
Returns the newly created `Booking` object.

**Permissions affect the visibility of certain fields:**
* The invoice_cost will be included if the permission `View invoice prices` is enabled.
* The invoice_cost will be null if `View invoice prices` is disabled.
* The total_cost will be included if the permission `View amounts` is enabled.
* The total_cost will be null if `View amounts` is disabled.

**invoice_cost** represents the invoice price of the customer type. 

**total_cost** represents the total cost of the customer type, including any priced custom fields.

Note: `total_cost` reflects only priced custom fields explicitly provided in the request; priced defaults are not applied automatically.

#### Priced custom fields (behavior change)

When the POST payload omits `custom_field_values`, the API will not auto-create values for priced custom fields. Only unpriced custom fields may be defaulted. Priced custom fields apply only when explicitly sent.

- Required fields: Required CFs remain required. If a required priced CF isn’t sent, the request fails with a validation error.
- What is “priced”? Any CF whose selection changes price (e.g., priced yes/no, or an extended option with a surcharge/discount).
- Unpriced CFs: Informational fields that do not change price. These may still be defaulted.

Example (omit priced CF → no price change):
```json
{
  "contact": { "name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com", "phone": "555-111-2222" },
  "customers": [{ "customer_type_rate": 12345 }]
}
```

Example (include priced CF → price applied):
```json
{
  "contact": { "name": "Jane", "email": "jane@example.com", "phone": "555-111-2222" },
  "customers": [
    {
      "customer_type_rate": 12345,
      "custom_field_values": [
        { "custom_field": 67890, "value": true }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

post.description:
Used to create a new booking. To create a booking, the API Partner must assemble 1) the request URL for the availability being booked, and 2) the booking request payload, with all the details of the booking they want to create.

When creating a booking, several properties about the availability and the booking request must hold or the booking will not be created:

* **Availability open** - The vendor may close availabilities at any time, and availabilities are automatically closed at various times based on the company's criteria.
* **Availability capacity** - There must be available capacity on the availability for all customers
* **Customer type rate capacities** - For each booked customer type rate there must be available capacity for each customer of that customer type.
* **Exclusivity** - If any customer type rate being booked is exclusive, then it must be the only customer type rate being booked.
* **Correct Customer Type Rates** - Each customer type rate being booked must actually belong to the availability being booked.
* **Orders enabled** - When creating an order, the vendor must have orders enabled in their FareHarbor dashboard.

### Transportation

To request transportation for a booking, specify a `Lodging` pk for the `lodging` property when creating the booking as shown in the example.

#### Pickup Information

When a lodging is specified during booking creation and transportation is available for the lodging, the booking response will include a `pickup` property that provides pickup information as shown in the example.

Note: if a lodging is specified and transportation is not available for the specified lodging (or any lodging), the `pickup` property will have a `null` value.

#### Arrival Information

Arrival information is intended for customers who are providing their own transportation. When a lodging is not provided during booking creation or transportation is not available for the specified lodging (or any lodging), arrival information may be provided. When arrival information is available, the booking response will include an `arrival` property that provides arrival information as shown in the example.

Note: the `arrival` property will have a `null` value if pickup information is available.

delete.description:
Used to cancel the specified booking by issuing a `DELETE` request.

**Things to Note:**
* IMPORTANT - The ability to cancel a booking via API is controlled by cancellation policy settings in FareHarbor. You can read about cancellation expectations [here](#tag/Cancellation-Policies).
* Regardless of cancellation policy settings, the FareHarbor API will allow cancellations within a 5 minute grace period after a booking is created.

**Requirements:**
* The booking must be cancelled at least 48 hours prior to start time (availability start_at property) to receive 100% refund.
* Any affiliate-collected payments for the booking must be fully refunded.
* Cancellations are allowed up until the start time. However, if the booking is cancelled less than 48 hours prior to start time, there is no refund.
* The requesting user must have permission to cancel bookings (granted on a per-company basis).
